description: capital city of Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Atlanta is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Georgia.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.
Great Migration, Great Migrations, or The Great Migration may refer to:

description: former United States professional baseball leagues
description_wiki: The Negro leagues were professional baseball leagues primarily in the United States comprising teams of African Americans.

entity: Golden State Mutual Life Insurance Company
surface_forms: Golden State Mutual Life Insurance Company
description_wiki: Golden State Mutual Life Insurance Company was once the largest black-owned insurance company in the western United States, founded by William Nickerson Jr.
Norman Houston is the name of:
William Nickerson Jr.

description: city and county seat of Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Oakland is a city in the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area in the U.S. state of California.
The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: canyon located in Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Chavez Ravine, or Chávez Ravine, is a shallow canyon in Los Angeles, California.
Samuel William Yorty (October 1, 1909 – June 5, 1998) was an American politician, attorney, and radio host from Los Angeles, California.

aliases: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: civil rights organization in the United States
description_wiki: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) is an American civil rights organization formed in 1909 as an interracial endeavor to advance justice for African Americans by a group including W. E. B. Du Bois, Mary White Ovington, Moorfield Storey, Ida B. Wells, Lillian Wald, Emil G. Hirsch and Henry Moskowitz.

H. Claude Hudson (1886–1989) was a prominent American businessman and advocate for civil rights, best known for helping to found the Broadway Federal Savings and Loan Association (now Broadway Federal Bank) in Los Angeles.

aliases: World Alliance of Young Men's Christian Associations
World's Alliance of Young Men's Christian Associations
description_wiki: YMCA, sometimes regionally called the Y, is a worldwide youth organization based in Vernier, Canton of Geneva, Switzerland, with more than 64 million beneficiaries in 120 countries.

aliases: National League on Urban Conditions Among Negroes
description_wiki: The National Urban League (NUL), formerly known as the National League on Urban Conditions Among Negroes, is a nonpartisan historic civil rights organization based in New York City that advocates on behalf of economic and social justice for African Americans and against racial discrimination in the United States.
Hollywood usually refers to:

description: president of the United States from 1981 to 1989 and actor (1911–2004)
description_wiki: Ronald Wilson Reagan (February 6, 1911 – June 5, 2004) was an American politician and actor who served as the 40th president of the United States from 1981 to 1989.

aliases: Republican Party of the United States
description_wiki: The Republican Party, commonly known as the Grand Old Party (GOP), is the major conservative and right-wing political party in the United States.

aliases: Democratic Party of the United States
description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.

description: president of the United States from 1969 to 1974 (1913–1994)
description_wiki: Richard Milhous Nixon (January 9, 1913 – April 22, 1994) was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974.

description: diplomat, educator, civil rights activist and the first African American Nobel Peace Prize winner (1904–1971)
description_wiki: Ralph Johnson Bunche ( BUNCH; August 7, 1904 – December 9, 1971) was an American (Black American) political scientist, diplomat, and leading actor in the mid-20th-century decolonization process and US civil rights movement, who received the 1950 Nobel Peace Prize for his late 1940s mediation in the Arab–Israeli conflict.
Jack Roosevelt Robinson (January 31, 1919 – October 24, 1972) was an American professional baseball player who was the first African American to play in Major League Baseball (MLB) in the modern era.
